slides.title: Your bedroom may be *aging your heart* at night
slides.sub: Noise you sleep right through can still stress your body. A quiet room is quietly protective.

slides.title: Sleep isn't always silent
slides.body: Traffic, a snorer or a humming street can register in your brain even when you *never fully wake up*.

slides.title: Micro-arousals add up
slides.body: Each sound can trigger a brief arousal. Your heart rate and blood pressure *can spike* for seconds at a time.

slides.title: Stress hormones rise
slides.body: Repeated nighttime noise is linked to higher cortisol and adrenaline, the same stress signals that *strain the heart*.

slides.title: It shapes how you age
slides.body: Chronic night noise is linked to higher blood pressure and cardiovascular risk, so a calm room *may help you age well*.

slides.title: Make the room quieter
slides.body: Earplugs, a fan or soft white noise can mask spikes. The goal is *steady, gentle* sound, not total silence.

slides.title: See how your nights *really score*
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so you can see whether a quieter room is paying off.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The noise you sleep through can still raise your blood pressure all night. 🌙😴

Micro-arousals from traffic, snoring or street sound can spike your heart rate and stress hormones, and over years that may shape how your heart ages. A quieter room is a simple, real upgrade. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep and habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why a Quiet Bedroom Protects Your Heart at Night
